reason: Two medium-impact flow omissions persist after Phase E: the 常駐処理業務エラー flow is missing its entire inbound handler chain and several error-path callbacks, and the プロセス異常停止 flow hides rollback behavior and BatchAction callbacks behind a vague「各ハンドラ error 経由」summary. Developers relying on these flows for error-handling design or debugging will receive an incomplete and partially misleading picture of how transactions and callbacks behave during failures. The hints_missing finding appears to have been resolved in Phase E (ThreadContextHandler_main is present in the Phase E hints array) and is not a true residual.

findings_assessment.location: s3 — 常駐処理業務エラー flow
findings_assessment.justification: The knowledge file shows only ProcessResidentHandler → MultiThreadExecutionHandler → BatchAction(callback) → BatchAction(error) → LoopHandler(error) → MultiThreadExecutionHandler(error) → ProcessResidentHandler(error) → RetryHandler(error). The entire inbound segment (DbConnectionManagementHandler_main, TransactionManagementHandler_main, RequestPathJavaPackageMapping, DbConnectionManagementHandler, LoopHandler, DataReadHandler, BatchAction inbound) is absent, as are the BatchAction callback after LoopHandler(error), DbConnectionManagementHandler(error), TransactionManagementHandler_main(error), and DbConnectionManagementHandler_main(error). A developer implementing or debugging resident-batch error handling—especially around transaction rollback scope and the BatchAction callback events—would form an incorrect mental model of the handler execution sequence. Phase E did not fix this: the regenerated section retained the same abbreviated structure, likely because the fix targeted only the hints array addition and left the flow prose unchanged.

findings_assessment.location: s3 — プロセス異常停止 flow
findings_assessment.justification: The knowledge file starts the abort flow at「各ハンドラ error 経由」, suppressing: (1) BatchAction (inbound) as the explicit trigger point where java.lang.Error is thrown, (2) LoopHandler (error) with its rollback-of-uncommitted-transactions detail, and (3) two BatchAction (callback) invocations during error propagation (after LoopHandler error and after MultiThreadExecutionHandler error). The rollback behavior of uncommitted transactions under a java.lang.Error is architecturally significant and differs from the RuntimeException path; omitting it means a developer investigating data-consistency guarantees on abnormal termination cannot rely solely on this knowledge file. Phase E did not fix this: the same condensed「各ハンドラ error 経由」wording remained in the regenerated output.

The Phase E output's index JSON for s3 explicitly includes "ThreadContextHandler_main" in the hints array. The finding appears to have been resolved during Phase E. Listing it as a residual finding is inconsistent with the Phase E artifact. No user-visible gap remains for this item.
